description: A dish of tortilla chips topped with cheese and other ingredients such as beans, jalapenos, salsa, guacamole, sour cream, or meat. They are usually baked or served hot so the cheese melts over the chips.
preparation_steps: Arrange tortilla chips in a layer.
Bake or heat until the cheese melts.
Finish with cold toppings such as salsa, guacamole, or sour cream if using.
Serve immediately while the chips are still crisp.

health_advice.benefits: Can include useful additions like beans, avocado, and salsa when prepared thoughtfully.
health_advice.risks: Often high in calories, sodium, and saturated fat, especially in loaded restaurant portions.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use moderate cheese, add beans or vegetables, and avoid making the portion too large.

dietary_notes.note: Many nachos can be vegetarian or vegan depending on the toppings. Tortilla chips are often gluten-free, but sauces and cross-contact can matter.

allergens: Possible gluten in some sauces or seasonings
